requirements.title: Wave30 Source Snapshot and Integrity Admission
requirements.implementation_action: Register every multipart archive part, logical ZIP hash, patch relationship, inventory count, CRC result, source defect, and authority ceiling without importing model authority.
requirements.acceptance: The five-part stream and patch are hash-bound; unsafe paths, missing parts, duplicate names, manifest inconsistencies, and the metadata-only authority ceiling are explicit.
requirements.review: archive manifest and inventory review

requirements.title: Discovery, Operational, and Empirical Authority Tiers
requirements.implementation_action: Separate source claims, normalized discovery metadata, installed operational assets, runtime observations, scoped certificates, and production selection authority.
requirements.acceptance: No Wave30 score, tag, copy-ready label, Civitai sample, or LLM statement can satisfy a runtime capability or promotion gate.
requirements.review: authority-matrix review

requirements.title: Model Asset Identity, Deduplication, and Version Lineage
requirements.implementation_action: Resolve family, model, version, file, SHA-256, duplicate group, supersession, preferred revision, and storage identity as separate immutable entities.
requirements.acceptance: Aliases converge on one content identity while distinct revisions remain independently testable and historically addressable.
requirements.review: dedupe and lineage fixture review

requirements.title: Model Lifecycle, Decision Authority, and Revocation State Machine
requirements.implementation_action: Define discovery, admitted, installed, load-proven, benchmark-candidate, provisionally-certified, production-certified, suspended, revoked, rejected, and superseded transitions.
requirements.acceptance: Every transition has one authority, evidence prerequisites, expiry, rollback, and immutable event history; an LLM cannot certify or promote.
requirements.review: state-transition and forbidden-authority tests

requirements.title: Strict Model Asset Intelligence Card
requirements.implementation_action: Normalize Wave30, Civitai, local, S3, EC2, ComfyUI, hash, loader, taxonomy, prompt, trigger, compatibility, rights, availability, and evidence fields into a strict versioned card.
requirements.acceptance: Each card distinguishes claimed, inferred, observed, measured, and certified facts and carries source-level citations and freshness.
requirements.review: schema, source crosswalk, and sample-card review

requirements.title: Capability, Role, Region, Modality, and Defect Ontology
requirements.implementation_action: Map checkpoints, LoRAs, adapters, controls, video, audio, speech, Foley, upscalers, and analyzers to normalized pass intents, targets, defects, controls, and risks.
requirements.acceptance: The selector can query exact functional meaning across character, scene, image, video, audio, and AV work without relying on filenames.
requirements.review: ontology coverage and ambiguity review

requirements.title: Family, Artifact, Revision, Bundle, and Certificate Separation
requirements.implementation_action: Model discovery operates at family level while execution, evidence, and certification bind exact artifact revisions and execution bundles.
requirements.acceptance: No family summary is used as artifact proof and no artifact certificate silently transfers to another hash, workflow, adapter bundle, or runtime.
requirements.review: cross-level leakage tests

requirements.title: Hybrid Retrieval Index and Immutable Citation Projection
requirements.implementation_action: Build structured filters, full-text search, embeddings, taxonomy joins, evidence joins, and materialized summaries over immutable source and empirical records.
requirements.acceptance: Every retrieved fact resolves to a versioned record and evidence reference; stale, conflicting, missing, and superseded records remain visible.
requirements.review: retrieval precision, freshness, conflict, and citation tests

requirements.title: Engine, Base-Family, Loader, and Adapter Hard Compatibility Graph
requirements.implementation_action: Represent exact compatible and incompatible edges for engines, checkpoints, LoRAs, VAEs, encoders, controls, schedulers, nodes, workflows, quantizations, and media adapters.
requirements.acceptance: Wrong-family assets and unproven cross-family assumptions fail before ranking with typed reasons.
requirements.review: positive and negative compatibility fixtures

requirements.title: Exact Model Execution Bundle Compiler
requirements.implementation_action: Compile one selectable unit from base model, LoRA stack, VAE, encoders, controls, workflow hash, node/runtime lock, sampler, precision, hardware, and prompt adapter.
requirements.acceptance: Selection and evidence bind the complete reproducible bundle rather than a model brand or standalone LoRA.
requirements.review: bundle reproducibility and substitution tests

requirements.title: LoRA and Component Interaction, Conflict, and Attribution Graph
requirements.implementation_action: Record pairwise and higher-order compatibility, dominance, cancellation, overcook, trigger collision, regional overlap, identity drift, and checkpoint dependence.
requirements.acceptance: Unknown combinations are not assumed safe; tested interactions are scoped and combinatorial testing is prioritized by use and risk.
requirements.review: designed-experiment and stack-ablation review

requirements.title: Availability, Residency, Resource, and Runtime Envelope
requirements.implementation_action: Bind local/S3/EC2 availability, bytes, load time, VRAM/RAM, precision, offload, warm-cache affinity, concurrency, and failure telemetry to each bundle.
requirements.acceptance: No route assumes an absent asset or starts outside a measured resource envelope; quality is not silently downgraded under pressure.
requirements.review: load, OOM, eviction, concurrency, and recovery tests

requirements.title: Progressive Model Qualification Funnel
requirements.implementation_action: Implement source admission, static scan, install/hash proof, loader smoke, baseline A/B, parameter sweep, capability benchmark, stack-interaction, shadow, and certification stages.
requirements.acceptance: Expensive rendering is reserved for eligible high-value or high-uncertainty candidates while every skipped stage and authority ceiling is explicit.
requirements.review: stage-entry, stop, resume, and failure tests

requirements.title: Canonical Multimodal Model Benchmark Corpus
requirements.implementation_action: Create fixed and held-out cases for character identity, anatomy, skin, hair, clothing, pose, interaction, environment, motion, speech, Foley, audio, and AV behavior.
requirements.acceptance: Cases bind approved inputs, baselines, seeds, prompts, masks, controls, expected effects, protected invariants, metrics, and adjudicated outcomes.
requirements.review: corpus coverage, leakage, and repeatability review

requirements.title: Parameter, Trigger, Prompt, Weight, Denoise, and Seed Sweep Engine
requirements.implementation_action: Generate reproducible baseline-controlled sweeps with early stopping, adaptive refinement, matched seeds, fixed workflows, and parameter-response curves.
requirements.acceptance: Best envelope, overcook threshold, instability, prompt sensitivity, and failure regions are measured without treating a single attractive output as proof.
requirements.review: paired sweep, early-stop, and reproducibility tests

requirements.title: Capability-Bucket Certificate, Expiry, and Requalification
requirements.implementation_action: Certify exact bundles only for measured pass intent, target, checkpoint, stack, character count, mask tier, resolution, workflow, runtime, and hardware buckets.
requirements.acceptance: Certificates include sample floors, confidence, hard-gate results, validity window, exclusions, fallback, and revocation links; there is no universal best-model certificate.
requirements.review: certificate boundary, expiry, and misuse tests

requirements.title: Canonical Model Selection Context Envelope
requirements.implementation_action: Compile character revisions, scene/shot/take, pass objective, defect, target/protected scope, modality, references, controls, masks, quality, runtime, cost, risk, and downstream needs.
requirements.acceptance: Equivalent requests canonicalize identically while materially different targets, owners, engines, stacks, or constraints remain separate evidence buckets.
requirements.review: context canonicalization and collision tests

requirements.title: Hard Eligibility Solver and Scalable Candidate Retrieval
requirements.implementation_action: Apply lifecycle, availability, engine, bundle, certificate, mask, control, character, resource, evidence-freshness, and prohibited-combination filters before ranking.
requirements.acceptance: The solver can search thousands of assets without scoring ineligible entries and returns complete typed exclusion evidence.
requirements.review: large-registry latency and zero-incompatible-selection tests

requirements.title: Conservative Contextual Evidence Ranker and Pareto Frontier
requirements.implementation_action: Rank eligible bundles with quality lower-confidence bounds, risk upper-confidence bounds, preservation, failures, resource cost, bridge cost, cache affinity, evidence freshness, and policy weights.
requirements.acceptance: Scores are replayable, calibrated, versioned, uncertainty-aware, and scoped; metadata priors cannot outrank measured production evidence.
requirements.review: offline replay, calibration, ranking-regret, and explanation tests

requirements.title: Exploration, Candidate Branch, Abstention, and Fallback Policy
requirements.implementation_action: Use bounded offline or shadow exploration for uncertain candidates, allow certified production champions for required passes, and emit abstention or explicit fallback when evidence is insufficient.
requirements.acceptance: Exploration never mutates an accepted parent or silently promotes a cold-start model; every candidate branch has budget, stop, QA, and learning eligibility.
requirements.review: cold-start, uncertainty, budget, fallback, and abstention tests

requirements.title: Per-Use Model and Bundle Observation Record
requirements.implementation_action: Record why a bundle was selected, exact context, parents, prompts, controls, outputs, telemetry, deterministic metrics, critic observations, defects, repair outcome, and final decision.
requirements.acceptance: Every execution produces an attributable report even when rejected, blocked, cancelled, or excluded from learning.
requirements.review: run-to-observation lineage tests

requirements.title: Contextual Model Performance Profile and Living Report Card
requirements.implementation_action: Aggregate eligible observations by exact capability bucket with sample counts, confidence intervals, response envelopes, success/failure modes, drift risks, recommended uses, and exclusions.
requirements.acceptance: Reports distinguish metadata priors, qualification trials, production observations, reviewer opinions, and certified facts and preserve all source evidence.
requirements.review: aggregation, confidence, provenance, and sparse-bucket tests

requirements.title: Append-Only Evidence Recalculation and Continual Learning Job
requirements.implementation_action: Recalculate profiles and ranking features from eligible immutable observations through versioned batch jobs rather than mutable online self-training.
requirements.acceptance: A production run cannot directly rewrite its own score; updates are reproducible, leakage-checked, reversible, and separated from holdout evaluation.
requirements.review: rebuild, idempotency, holdout leakage, and rollback tests

requirements.title: Behavior Drift, Regression, Suspension, Revocation, and Rollback
requirements.implementation_action: Detect model, workflow, runtime, dependency, prompt-template, data, reviewer, and workload drift and bind affected certificates, routes, cached results, and fallbacks.
requirements.acceptance: Critical drift suspends new selection immediately; requalification or rollback restores authority without rewriting prior decisions.
requirements.review: drift injection, blast-radius, revocation, and rollback tests

requirements.title: Autonomous Planner, Prompt, Router-Advisor, Reviewer, and Summarizer Role Contracts
requirements.implementation_action: Define bounded planner, prompt composer, retrieval analyst, router advisor, defect classifier, VLM critic, audio critic, report writer, and summarizer inputs and outputs.
requirements.acceptance: Every role has exact authority, tools, context budget, schemas, escalation, and prohibited actions; no role promotes its own proposal or observation.
requirements.review: role-routing and forbidden-authority tests

requirements.title: Registry-Grounded RAG, Evidence Bundle, and Context Memory Contract
requirements.implementation_action: Retrieve only versioned packages, model cards, certificates, benchmark results, failures, current run state, and policy records with citations, freshness, conflict, and compaction metadata.
requirements.acceptance: The LLM sees a bounded evidence packet rather than the whole library; missing or conflicting evidence causes uncertainty, alternatives, or abstention rather than invention.
requirements.review: retrieval, injection, stale evidence, conflict, and context-limit tests

requirements.title: Schema-Constrained Proposal, Prompt Package, Tool Gateway, and Policy Decision
requirements.implementation_action: Require typed planner proposals, prompt packages, reviewer observations, tool actions, policy decisions, uncertainty, evidence IDs, alternatives, and denied-action records.
requirements.acceptance: Invalid IDs, unsupported claims, arbitrary paths, unallowlisted workflows, credential access, registry mutation, and promotion attempts fail deterministically.
requirements.review: malformed output, hallucinated ID, path, injection, and authorization tests

requirements.title: Exact Self-Hosted Role Stack, Benchmark, Shadow, and Activation Control
requirements.implementation_action: Register model, revision, runtime, quantization, chat template, parser, context, batching, hardware, fallback, and role-specific benchmark certificates.
requirements.acceptance: Only a role-qualified exact stack activates; planner and reviewer changes run in shadow, preserve prior decisions, and cannot silently change route or promotion behavior.
requirements.review: held-out role, load, failover, shadow, drift, and rollback proof

requirements.title: Deterministic, Perceptual, VLM, Audio-Critic, and Playback QA Ensemble
requirements.implementation_action: Evaluate technical validity, effect accuracy, target fidelity, identity, anatomy, preservation, mask leakage, temporal behavior, audio quality, sync, resource stability, and lineage.
requirements.acceptance: Hard facts remain deterministic, critics emit scoped observations with uncertainty, and no single scalar or reviewer overrides a hard failure.
requirements.review: adjudicated image, video, audio, and AV panels

requirements.title: Baseline, Counterfactual, Ablation, and Stack Attribution Protocol
requirements.implementation_action: Compare no-adapter baselines, matched seeds, component ablations, alternative bundles, strength curves, and protected outputs to isolate actual model contribution.
requirements.acceptance: A model receives credit or blame only where the experiment can attribute the change; checkpoint, prompt, seed, mask, and stack confounding are recorded.
requirements.review: paired comparison, ablation, and confound tests

requirements.title: Critic Calibration, Disagreement, Bias, and Adjudication Control
requirements.implementation_action: Measure false accept/reject rates, region and modality coverage, reviewer-version effects, uncertainty calibration, disagreement, and escalation against held-out adjudicated cases.
requirements.acceptance: Reviewer observations remain candidate evidence until calibration and policy authorize their exact use; disagreement is retained rather than averaged away.
requirements.review: blinded held-out calibration and disagreement tests

requirements.title: Selection Decision Audit, Model Report, and Promotion Gate
requirements.implementation_action: Package candidate exclusions, rank features, uncertainty, selected bundle, execution, QA, comparison, observation, performance delta, certificate, and policy outcome.
requirements.acceptance: Every future run can explain why a model was selected, how it behaved, what changed in its report, and whether the evidence may affect future selection.
requirements.review: complete decision-packet replay and audit

requirements.title: Event Store, Evidence Store, Feature Store, and Projection Boundaries
requirements.implementation_action: Persist source records, lifecycle events, bundles, trials, observations, certificates, reports, ranking features, decisions, and audit projections with immutable IDs and snapshots.
requirements.acceptance: State reconstructs from append-only events, feature values resolve to evidence, and no mutable cache becomes authority.
requirements.review: empty-projection rebuild, hash, idempotency, and corruption tests

requirements.title: Resource-Aware Batch Qualification and Active-Learning Scheduler
requirements.implementation_action: Prioritize installed, high-value, high-usage, high-risk, coverage-gap, and uncertain candidates; schedule sweeps, comparisons, and requalification under GPU, storage, time, and cost budgets.
requirements.acceptance: The scheduler avoids all-pairs explosion, respects leases and thermal/resource envelopes, resumes safely, and records why work was prioritized or deferred.
requirements.review: priority, fairness, budget, crash, cancellation, and resume tests

requirements.title: Model Explorer, Qualification Queue, Comparison, Report, and Route-Explanation UX
requirements.implementation_action: Expose library health, source authority, availability, compatibility, test coverage, certificates, reports, failures, drift, decisions, and side-by-side media without raw node or credential exposure.
requirements.acceptance: Operators can trace every claim and approve policy changes or exceptions without manually editing graphs or registry bytes.
requirements.review: scripted operator journeys and accessibility review

requirements.title: Telemetry, Capacity, Security, Backup, Recovery, and Degraded-Mode Control
requirements.implementation_action: Monitor ingestion, index freshness, queue depth, load failures, QA latency, ranking drift, certificate health, storage, costs, untrusted metadata, model scans, tools, and recovery points.
requirements.acceptance: Outage or saturation changes routes only through declared policies; source text and model files cannot obtain tool, credential, registry, or promotion authority.
requirements.review: outage, restore, saturation, injection, and least-authority tests

requirements.title: Wave30 Dry-Run Import, Reconciliation, and Exception Report
requirements.implementation_action: Map all 7,282 artifacts and 3,770 families into discovery records, reconcile 675-member archive defects and selector/status disagreements, and emit no operational promotion.
requirements.acceptance: Counts, hashes, duplicates, missing fields, blocked rows, stale reports, absent visual-test assets, and migration mappings reconcile or produce typed exceptions.
requirements.review: full dry-run counts, schema, and exception reconciliation

requirements.title: Copy-Ready and High-Value Pilot Qualification Tranche
requirements.implementation_action: Hash-verify and qualify the 187 Wave30 copy-ready candidates plus a stratified set of installed/high-value models across priority capability buckets.
requirements.acceptance: The pilot produces real bundles, A/B evidence, reports, scoped certificates, failures, cost measurements, and rollback without treating copy-ready as production-ready.
requirements.review: real pilot render/playback and evidence review

requirements.title: Value, Coverage, Risk, and Uncertainty-Driven Library Expansion
requirements.implementation_action: Expand qualification by production demand, capability gaps, model diversity, uncertainty, expected value of information, and resource budget rather than archive order.
requirements.acceptance: Coverage grows measurably while unqualified long-tail assets remain discoverable, on-demand-testable, and excluded from certified production routes.
requirements.review: coverage, regret, cost, drift, and long-tail on-demand tests

requirements.title: Autonomous Model Intelligence Release Certification and Main-Task Adoption
requirements.implementation_action: Certify schemas, stores, ingestion, bundles, qualification, selection, LLM roles, QA, reports, App UX, recovery, security, rollback, and preservation handoff.
requirements.acceptance: Rows221-259 are traceable; runtime claims are evidence-backed; no critical gate is open; the main task formally adopts or rejects every additive artifact.
requirements.review: independent end-to-end selection, generation, QA, report, recovery, and rollback review
